rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al VS. HOWARD MULLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANTs CHARLENE ATTARD, WILLIAM FACENDINI & FERRYLE MCOMBER's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. On March 1, 2013, the Court granted this motion for good faith settlement, and continued the hearing for supplemental briefing on the issue of whether the breach of fiduciary duty claim in the Mullins' Third Amended Cross Complaint should be dismissed in light of that ruling. The request for dismissal of the Mullins' Third Amended Cross Complaint is granted as to the first, fourth and sixth causes of action. The request for dismissal is denied without prejudice as to the Fifth Cause of Action for breach of fiduciary duty. Defendants may raise the effect of the Court's finding of a good faith settlement on the Fifth Cause of Action with the trial judge. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT NOVARTIS PHARMACEUTICALS CORPORATION's Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the Novartis motion, but both sides are to appear. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cournale & Co. (Cal Dre License No. 00322534), Clyde Cournale, Curtis Cournale, William Schmetterer'S Motion To Compel Designated Experts To Appear For Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 26, Defendants Cournale & Co. (Cal Dre License No. 00322534), Clyde Cournale, Curtis Cournale, William Schmetterer'S Motion To Compel Designated Experts To Appear For Deposition. Granted. The depositions of expert witnesses John Wallace and Steven Littlefield shall take place as soon as practicable after the deposition of Plaintiff now scheduled for April 16 and 17, 2013. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith as to dates for these expert witness depositions. In the event the parties cannot agree on dates before this case is called on March 18, 2013, each party shall be prepared to provide at the hearing two proposed dates for each deposition when they (and/or their witness, if applicable) are available. The Court will order specific dates for these two expert depositions at the hearing if the parties cannot agree. If the parties are able to agree on deposition dates in advance of the hearing, they may notify the court and submit a stipulation and proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org to avoid the necessity of a court appearance. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHERISH M. SMITH, AS AN INDIVIDUAL, AND ON BEHALF VS. NOR-CAL BEVERAGE CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Adnmotion To Quash Defts Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To Nationwide Legal Services
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHERISH M. SMITH, AS AN INDIVIDUAL, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED'S motion To Quash Defts Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To Nationwide Legal Services Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Deny the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 27, PLAINTIFF JUVENTINO ESTRADA's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents. Granted in part and denied in part. Request Nos. 13-15: Granted to the extent that National Union shall provide a written verification that it has provided all responsive documents within its possession, custody or control that it has been able to locate following a diligent search. As Request No. 5 and request nos. 19-25, 27-28: Denied. The Master Policy has been produced. The low relevance of the requested documents is outweighed by the burden to National Union of providing these additional documents, without any real basis to do so. The remainder of the motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded to either party. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: SARINE VOLTAGE VS. SFMTA/MUNI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 879. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN RAFAEL APONTE VS. BANK OF AMERICA et al (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 18, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING REQUIRED. The demurrer is continued to May 6, 2013. The Plaintiff needs to serve a copy of his response to the demurrer on defense counsel. Plaintiff also needs to provide a courtesy copy of his response with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Please direct the response directly to department 302. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
